text: Question 1(a)(i): Draw -135 degrees and find its quadrant.
Since the angle is negative, we rotate clockwise by 135 degrees.
This lands in the third quadrant. So, -135 degrees lies in Quadrant III.
Question 1(b): Find another positive angle co-terminal with -135 degrees.
Rotating counterclockwise to the same terminal arm, we get 360 minus 135, which is 225 degrees.
Question 1(a)(ii): Draw 740 degrees and find its quadrant.
Since 740 is positive, we rotate counterclockwise. 740 degrees is two full rounds of 360, plus 20 degrees.
This lands in the first quadrant. So, 740 degrees lies in Quadrant I.
